title: cinchona ovata var. vulgaris
intro: Cinchona ovata var. vulgaris, also known as the lesser cinchona or bitter cinchona, is a variety of the cinchona plant that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties, attributed to the presence of quinine. The health benefits of cinchona ovata var. vulgaris include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of malaria, and soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the bark of the cinchona plant is typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
